title: hypericum maculatum subsp. undulatum
intro: Hypericum maculatum subsp. undulatum, also known as spotted St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant within the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its unique spotted leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, such as wound healing and immune system support. The leaves and flowers of the plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, anxiety, and insomnia. Additionally, Hypericum maculatum subsp. undulatum is also used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels.
